summary: I read the ask and the draft requirements, then grounded the questions in the actual data model. Two facts shaped the list: **workers are a global/shared pool** (no project association), which makes "worker fleet health" inherently un-scoped unlike the rest of the multi-project dashboard; and there is **no activity/event table**, so "recent activity" must be assembled from existing records (approvals, manager decisions, rework events, completed step runs, pipeline transitions). I wrote ten numbered open questions covering the decisions where human context materially changes the build — fleet scoping and the health/staleness threshold, the role-match check for stranded work, the activity feed's source and window, the definition of "needs attention," pagination limits, whether to offer a project filter, the live-update-vs-periodic-refresh split, and whether the dashboard becomes the app root — each with an assumed default so the gate reviewer only has to respond where a default is wrong. Output written to `.pipeliner/phases/01-define/main/clarifying-questions-writer/output/open_questions.md`.
